message:
Fix shellcheck warnings in acfs.zshrc

- Add shellcheck directives for zsh-specific variables (SC2034)
- Disable SC1091 for dynamic source paths
- Fix mkcd function to properly return on cd failure (SC2164)
- Add comments explaining shellcheck suppressions

The file already met all task requirements:
- No duplicate PATH/Bun blocks
- Conditional tool aliases
- Safe chpwd hook for ls after cd
- Atuin path set before init
- Local overrides via ~/.zshrc.local

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.6","title":"Create coding agents installer (scripts/lib/agents.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Coding Agents Installer

## Description
Build a library to install the three coding agent CLIs.

## Agents to Install

### Claude Code
```bash
# Official installer (check docs for latest)
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
# Or via installer script
```
- Installs to ~/.local/bin/claude
- Requires login after install

### Codex CLI (OpenAI)
```bash
bun install -g @openai/codex@latest
```
- Installed via bun global
- Requires API key or login

### Gemini CLI (Google)
```bash
bun install -g @google/gemini-cli@latest
```
- Installed via bun global
- Requires login

## Aliases
These are set in acfs.zshrc:
```bash
alias cc='NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=32768" ENABLE_BACKGROUND_TASKS=1 claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'
alias cod='codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -m gpt-5.2-codex -c model_reasoning_effort="xhigh" -c model_reasoning_summary_format=experimental --enable web_search_request'
alias gmi='gemini --yolo --model gemini-3-pro-preview'
```

## Functions

```bash
install_claude_code()  # Install Claude CLI
install_codex_cli()    # Install Codex CLI
install_gemini_cli()   # Install Gemini CLI
verify_agents()        # Check all installed
```

## Note
These require login after install - that happens in onboarding.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 3 agents installed
- [ ] Commands available in PATH
- [ ] Aliases configured in zshrc
- [ ] Verification commands work
- [ ] Clear message about login requirement","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:25:22.66693-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:56:10.596544-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:56:10.596544-05:00","close_reason":"Created scripts/lib/agents.sh with install_claude_code, install_codex_cli, install_gemini_cli, and verify_agents functions.  {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.7","title":"Create Dicklesworthstone stack installer (scripts/lib/stack.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Dicklesworthstone Stack Installer

## Description
Build a library to install all 8 Dicklesworthstone tools.

## Tools to Install (in order)

### 1. NTM (Named Tmux Manager)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/install.sh | bash
```
- Agent orchestration cockpit
- Verify: `ntm --help`

### 2. MCP Agent Mail
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail/main/scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --yes
```
- Agent coordination server
- Verify: `command -v am`

### 3. Ultimate Bug Scanner (UBS)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scanner/master/install.sh | bash -s -- --easy-mode
```
- Bug scanning with guardrails
- Verify: `ubs --help`

### 4. Beads Viewer (BV)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/main/install.sh | bash
```
- Task management TUI
- Verify: `bv --help`

### 5. CASS (Coding Agent Session Search)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search/main/install.sh | bash -s -- --easy-mode --verify
```
- Unified session search
- Verify: `cass --help`

### 6. CM (CASS Memory System)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system/main/install.sh | bash -s -- --easy-mode --verify
```
- Procedural memory for agents
- Verify: `cm --version`

### 7. CAAM (Coding Agent Account Manager)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_account_manager/main/install.sh | bash
```
- Auth switching
- Verify: `caam --help`

### 8. SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/slb/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
```
- Two-person rule for dangerous commands
- Verify: `slb --help`

## Functions

```bash
install_ntm()
install_mcp_agent_mail()
install_ubs()
install_bv()
install_cass()
install_cm()
install_caam()
install_slb()
verify_stack()  # Check all 8 installed
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 8 tools installed
- [ ] All commands in PATH
- [ ] All verify commands pass

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.8","title":"Create main install.sh orchestrator","description":"# Task: Create Main install.sh Orchestrator

## Description
Build the main installer script that orchestrates all phases.

## Script Structure

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Parse arguments
parse_args() {
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case $1 in
            --yes) ACFS_AUTO_APPROVE=1 ;;
            --mode) ACFS_MODE="$2"; shift ;;
            --dry-run) ACFS_DRY_RUN=1 ;;
            --quiet) ACFS_QUIET=1 ;;
            --verbose) ACFS_VERBOSE=1 ;;
            *) log_error "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
        esac
        shift
    done
}

# Main phases
main() {
    log_step "0/8" "Preflight checks"
    source_libs
    validate_os
    
    log_step "1/8" "Setting up ubuntu user"
    ensure_ubuntu_user
    setup_passwordless_sudo
    
    log_step "2/8" "Creating workspace"
    create_workspace
    
    log_step "3/8" "Installing shell environment"
    install_zsh_environment
    
    log_step "4/8" "Installing languages"
    install_languages
    
    log_step "5/8" "Installing dev tools"
    install_dev_tools
    
    log_step "6/8" "Installing coding agents"
    install_agents
    
    log_step "7/8" "Installing Dicklesworthstone stack"
    install_stack
    
    log_step "8/8" "Final configuration"
    deploy_configs
    install_onboard
    
    print_completion_message
}

main "$@"
```

## Error Handling
- Trap errors with helpful message
- Log to file before exit
- Suggest running acfs doctor

## Checkpointing
- Write completed phases to ~/.acfs/state.json
- Skip completed phases on re-run
- Support --force to re-run all

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 8 phases implemented
- [ ] Clean error handling
- [ ] Checkpointing works
- [ ] --dry-run mode
- [ ] Completion message with next steps
- [ ] Works as root or ubuntu","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:25:56.003488-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.347345-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.347345-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.8","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:25:56.005402-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.9","title":"Create clean canonical acfs.zshrc","description":"# Task: Create Clean Canonical acfs.zshrc

## Description
Create the production-ready zshrc that ACFS deploys to VPS.

## File: acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc

## Requirements
Based on user's original zshrc but:
- Remove duplicate PATH/Bun blocks
- Remove duplicate aliases
- Use safe chpwd hook instead of overriding cd()
- Fix atuin path before init ordering
- Conditional tool aliases (only if tool exists)
- Support user overrides via ~/.zshrc.local

## Structure

```bash
# ~/.acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc
# ACFS canonical zsh config (managed)

# --- SSH guard ---
# Prevent weird terminal settings over SSH

# --- Paths (early) ---
# ~/.local/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, go, bun, atuin

# --- Oh My Zsh ---
# Theme: powerlevel10k
# Plugins: git, autosuggestions, syntax-highlighting, etc.

# --- Editor preference ---
# vim over SSH, nvim locally

# --- Modern CLI aliases ---
# Only set if tool exists
# lsd/eza, bat, fd, rg, dust, btop, nvim, lazygit

# --- Git aliases ---
# gs, gd, gdc, gp, gpu, gco, gcm, gca, gb, glog

# --- Docker aliases ---
# dc, dps, dpsa, dimg, dex

# --- Directory shortcuts ---
# dev, proj, dots, p

# --- Custom functions ---
# mkcd, extract

# --- Safe ls after cd ---
# Using chpwd hook, not overriding cd()

# --- Tool settings ---
# UV_LINK_MODE, etc.

# --- Tool initializations ---
# cargo, atuin, zoxide, direnv, fzf

# --- Agent aliases ---
# cc, cod, gmi (dangerous mode enabled)

# --- Keybindings ---
# Ctrl/Alt+Arrow for word movement
# Home/End keys
# Force atuin Ctrl-R
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No duplicate code
- [ ] All tools alias only if present
- [ ] chpwd hook works correctly
- [ ] Atuin Ctrl-R works
- [ ] Local overrides supported

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5vz","title":"Add installer security verification","description":"# Task: Add Installer Security Verification

## Description
Make the curl|bash pattern more trustworthy.

## Features

### 1. Checksum Verification
Before running sub-installers, verify checksums:
```bash
verify_checksum() {
    local url="$1"
    local expected_sha256="$2"
    local content=$(curl -fsSL "$url")
    local actual=$(echo "$content" | sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1)
    if [[ "$actual" != "$expected_sha256" ]]; then
        log_error "Checksum mismatch for $url"
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "$content"
}
```

### 2. HTTPS Enforcement
All URLs must be HTTPS. Fail on HTTP.

### 3. --print Mode
Show exactly what will be downloaded:
```bash
install.sh --print
# Outputs:
# Will download:
# - https://bun.sh/install (sha256: abc123...)
# - https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh (sha256: def456...)
# ...
```

### 4. Source Links
Website shows link to install.sh source on GitHub.

### 5. Changelog
What changed since last version.

## Checksums File
```yaml
# checksums.yaml
installers:
  bun: "abc123..."
  uv: "def456..."
  # etc
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checksum verification for key installers
- [ ] HTTPS enforced
- [ ] --print mode works
- [ ] Website links to source","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:39:05.141855-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:39:05.141855-05:00","labels":["installer","security","task"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","title":"EPIC: acfs doctor & Update Commands","description":"# Epic: acfs doctor & Update Commands

## Overview
Build the diagnostic and update commands that help users verify and maintain their ACFS installation.

## Background & Reasoning
Beginners don't debug - they need clear "is it working?" feedback and simple fixes.

### acfs doctor
Single command to verify entire installation:
- Identity (user ubuntu, passwordless sudo)
- Shell setup (zsh, plugins, theme)
- All tools installed and working
- Clear PASS/FAIL/WARN for each check

### acfs update
Keep everything current:
- apt update + upgrade
- Update bun globals (codex, gemini, vercel, etc.)
- Optionally re-run tool installers

## Doctor Output Spec

```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
User: ubuntu ✅
Mode: vibe (passwordless sudo: enabled) ✅
OS: Ubuntu 25.x ✅

Shell
  PASS ✅ zsh installed (5.x)
  PASS ✅ oh-my-zsh installed
  ...

Dicklesworthstone stack
  PASS ✅ ntm (available)
  PASS ✅ slb (available)
  ...

Summary: 34 passed, 0 failed, 1 warning
Next: run `onboard`
```

## JSON Mode
`acfs doctor --json` outputs structured data for website integration:
```json
{
  "checks": [
    {"id": "identity.user_is_ubuntu", "status": "pass", ...}
  ],
  "summary": {"pass": 34, "warn": 1, "fail": 0}
}
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] acfs doctor checks all ~40 tools
- [ ] Each check has stable ID for website mapping
- [ ] Exit code 0 = all pass, 1 = any fail
- [ ] --json mode for automation
- [ ] acfs update works without interaction
- [ ] Clear fix suggestions for each failure","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:55.795457-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:55.795457-05:00","labels":["epic","tooling"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.1","title":"Implement acfs doctor command","description":"# Task: Implement acfs doctor Command

## Description
Build the diagnostic command that verifies the entire ACFS installation.

## Location
Install to: `~/.acfs/bin/acfs`
Subcommand: `acfs doctor`

## Output Format

```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
User: ubuntu ✅
Mode: vibe (passwordless sudo: enabled) ✅
OS: Ubuntu 25.x ✅
Workspace: /data/projects ✅

Shell
  PASS ✅ zsh installed (zsh 5.x)
  PASS ✅ oh-my-zsh installed
  ...

Summary: 34 passed, 0 failed, 1 warning
Next: run `onboard`
```

## Check Structure

Each check has:
- id: Stable identifier for website mapping
- label: Human-readable name
- status: pass | warn | fail
- details: Version or extra info
- fix: Command to fix (on failure)

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash

run_check() {
    local id="$1"
    local label="$2"
    local check_cmd="$3"
    local fix_cmd="$4"
    
    if eval "$check_cmd" &>/dev/null; then
        echo "PASS ✅ $label"
        echo "{\"id\":\"$id\",\"status\":\"pass\"}" >> "$JSON_OUTPUT"
    else
        echo "FAIL ❌ $label"
        echo "      Fix: $fix_cmd"
        echo "{\"id\":\"$id\",\"status\":\"fail\",\"fix\":\"$fix_cmd\"}" >> "$JSON_OUTPUT"
        ((FAIL_COUNT++))
    fi
}

# Run all checks
run_check "identity.user_is_ubuntu" "Logged in as ubuntu" \
    "[[ \$(whoami) == 'ubuntu' ]]" \
    "sudo -u ubuntu bash"
```

## JSON Mode
`acfs doctor --json` outputs:
```json
{
  "acfs_version": "0.1.0",
  "checks": [...],
  "summary": {"pass": 34, "warn": 1, "fail": 0}
}
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: All pass (warnings OK)
- 1: Any failure
- 2: Doctor crashed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All ~40 checks implemented
- [ ] Stable check IDs
- [ ] --json mode works
- [ ] Correct exit codes
- [ ] Fix suggestions on failure
- [ ] Summary at end","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:41.266691-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:41.266691-05:00","labels":["task","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:41.268261-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -1,5 +1,9 @@
+# shellcheck shell=bash disable=SC2034,SC1091
 # ~/.acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc
 # ACFS canonical zsh config (managed). Safe, fast, minimal duplication.
+#
+# SC2034: ZSH_THEME, plugins, PROMPT, RPROMPT are used by zsh/omz (not bash)
+# SC1091: Dynamic source paths can't be followed by shellcheck
 
 # --- SSH stty guard (prevents weird remote terminal settings) ---
 if [[ -n "$SSH_CONNECTION" ]]; then
@@ -125,7 +129,7 @@ alias search='apt search'
 alias uca='claude update && bun install -g @openai/codex@latest && bun install -g @google/gemini-cli@latest'
 
 # --- Custom functions ---
-mkcd() { mkdir -p "$1" && cd "$1"; }
+mkcd() { mkdir -p "$1" && cd "$1" || return; }
 
 extract() {
   if [[ -f "$1" ]]; then
